item.title: I just had a surgey on the 17th that went bad — 34 replies — 136.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
I just had a surgey on the 17th that went bad

Open thread • Replies: 34 • Updated: 2026-04-23 21:57 UTC

OP

It was suppose to be a minor incision to fix a rare genetic anomaly in my left kidney, I woke up in the ICU and couldn't move, I was told there were complications and they had to change the surgey to a kidney bypass, which required opening my entire chest from my groin to my sternum.
I was in critical condition and nearly died a few times from my body going haywire, and the surgey scar twisting and spasming in the worst pain I ever experienced.

I'm still recovering now but still in intense pain, I got out of the hospital as soon as I was able to, because the nurses were not giving me pain medicine in fear I'd overdose so I was just sitting helpless.

The doctors who performed it and hospital gave me the lowest dose lortab they could for 4 days and sent me on my way, and ignoring my requests for something stronger as I can't even sleep.

Should I sue the hospital and doctor for this?
(This is a business related discussion)

Early replies (first 3)

62148874
>>62148853
Fuck yea. At very least someone owes you a discussion for what went wrong. Financially speaking. Theres a stat out there you know where hospital botches and deaths skyrocket a certain time each year when all the new medical students join the workforce. Best to not get sick around that time.

62148883
>>62148853
>that will be 20k in medical bills goykun

62148887
>>62148874
He said my renal vein was so crushed between my spine it was unusable so they had to use a synthetic graft to connect it to my heart to my kidney.
I'm not happy about it, I don't care so much about the scar, I'm not thrilled I'd got basically a piece of PVC in my body that only lasts 10 years typically which means I might have to have it done again.
I'm not even out of the woodwork the entire area bloated up yesterday and I haven't ate in 8 days.

He was an experienced vascular surgeon but my condition is extremely rare so there's not many surgeries on it each one being different.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62148953
Did you forget to tip?

62148956
>>62148853
Yes.
>>62148887
I feel sorry, but are you by any chance obese?
lmfao, you are almost dead. Enjoy your life to the best. Also before you die, could you give me your crypto or assets?

62149003
>>62148956
I'm broke and on medicaid so luckily I won't have to pay anything
>>62148949
I spent 4 years trying to get disability and denied because my condition is so rare they didn't accept it all the way to the federal level.

62149036
>>62148949
It was like a horror movie, my core muscles were gone they cut through my abdomen with scissors, I had no core muscles to move anything and my shoulders felt like someone drove shivs through them if I tried to get up.
Then they were giving me hydromorphine and every time I nearly threw up which would have probably killed me luckily the first nurse knew better and when I showed signs of throwing up she hit me with anti nausea medicine, but the pain was so intense I needed it every 2 hours, but then every nurse shift would do things "their own way" 2 nurses I had to kick out of my room, one was a black nurse who I think was slipping the vials and not injecting me because I rapidly sobered up and was watching watch she was doing because i needed the shit badly.
Soon as I could i forced down their shit food and forced a bowel movement which was the worst pain beyond the initial pain, once I did that they let me leave but with fucking 10mg hydros, should have been oxycodone minimal my god they dissected me.

62149051
>>62148956
And no I'm not obese I'm 6'4 220lbs

62149057
>>62149036
Nigga got dissected and the first thing he does is make a frogpost about it

62149066
>>62149057
https://youtu.be/9g_mZ6cEAXI

62149072
>>62149057
Oddly nobody wants to talk to me, even family, somehow me nearly dying caused an effect where not a single person gives a shit, so I mostly just wanted to get this experience off my chest since well nobody wants to talk.
I'm about to sleep actually if I can every time I try I wake right back up.

62149079
Good lucky anon.
Italian here

62149084
>>62149072
That sucks man LOL. Yeah I kind of get it though, it's like you're so unfortunate nobody wants to acknowledge your existence because it makes them too sad. Guess the web is made for moments like these.

>>62149066
This is tripping me out man

62149090
>>62148853
Post chest scar and timestamp

62149098
>>62149079
Thanks pizzaman
>>62149084
Odd I have a close friend I always talk to, and its like he disappeared, my sister and nephew who i knew were pieces of shit didn't even care to show/text which is fine It's a good excuse to change some of my surroundings, I guess.
>>62149084
Goodnight anons Im getting the spins from the opiates gonna lay down.

62149104
>>62149090
for what? it's under a vacuum pump right now
probably for money I would but I just coughed and felt like I got cut open again, i'm laying down money doesn't mean shit to me right now, nothing does well friendship kinda but whatever.

62149119
>>62149090
to satisfy your morbid curiosity

62149124
>>62149098
Good night anon, hope something good happens. Maybe you should sue them. They have malpractice insurance anyway so probably wouldn't even hurt them too much & then you could at least become an investor and have fun.

62149133
>>62149124
>the spins wearing off a bit they come and go
But if I stop responding you'll know I ran off.
just took off the compression band I'm using and it kills, it kills with it on and kills with it off.
Kinda what I was thinking they have insurance, this same hospital who did a shit job of care also missed this issue when I complained about my kidney feeling damaged 7 years prior, missed it multiple times.

62149147
>>62149124
They kept checking my stomach to hear if gas was moving since they cut my lower bowel, I knew something was up but going back and reading the report of exactly what they did.

62149716
>>62148853
Sorry to hear about your harrowing experience. At least you can take solace in the fact that all this happened to an anti-semite instead of a decent human being.

62149726
The forms you signed before the surgery included a promise not to sue, but you might as well talk to a lawyer.

62149729
>>62149726
Well it was suppose to also be a same day surgery and minimall invasive and not putting a fucking 8mm PVC tube inside me.

62149731
>>62149716
I know, right? What kind of heckin douche canoe sues doctors? Science H. Christ, you're goycattle! You're not allowed to heckin be rude to our masters.

62149737
>>62148853
You need an attorney.

62149740
>>62149119
Jesus christ anon, sorry you're going through this.

62149745
>>62149740
The most painful experience of my entire life, I'm not sure what else could compare to the pain I felt those first 3 days, and frankly the pain I feel now though each day it's going down, I'm scared to take off the bandage tomorrow, it doesn't feel healed at all.

62149751
>>62149119
You also need to lose some weight. Stop eating Burger King and switch to paleo or keto diet for a while.

62149754
>>62149119
HOLY SHIT

62149758
>>62149751
Steroid use, I also have a plasma disorder that makes my blood attack itself, I've been on prednisone for 3 years now, without it I'll waste away like I'm doing now 8 days and pretty sure I'm down 15lbs or more.
Before steroids I was 170lbs and you could see my ribs.

62149764
>>62149119
Holy Christ brother I'm sorry. Sue them to fucking death until those niggers are on the street. You absolutely need to contact a medical claims attorney. Godspeed to you.

62149770
>>62149764
It's alright I guess, yeah I plan on talking to some about this mess, I like my surgeon and I don't even think he did a bad job but it wasn't what we discussed and had this been a "possible scenario" I would have lived with the pain like I had been for my whole life.

62149782
>>62149764
First thing I told my doctor when he arrived in my room "probably because I was yelling at the nurses I'm going to sue them"
Was "What the hell did you do to me!"
his response was "Dude I had my hands inside your soul"
Then I passed out.

item.title: May need to declare bankrupcy due to severe schizo symptoms, advice? — 29 replies — 116.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
May need to declare bankrupcy due to severe schizo symptoms, advice?

Open thread • Replies: 29 • Updated: 2026-04-23 22:05 UTC

OP

I'm worried about losing my pets and gaming pc and gaming console, it's all I have left. Does anyone know about bankruptcy or has anyone filed bankrupcy? I need advice I don't want to pay a lawyer yet or talk to a lawyer yet especially I don't want to tell a lawyer I have pets and a gaming PC and a console.

I need to file chapter 7 bankruptcy in California because I had what doctors call a schizophrenic episode where I got visions of what I thought was God talking to me and I destroyed all my property after I maxxed out my credit cards and destroyed everything almost (including old gaming PC) and changed all the passwords on my accounts. I haven't even signed into my bank account online thing in 4+ years and haven't used my debit card in 4 years. I experienced incarceration during this time and 4 years of homelessness.

I have 2 lizards lizard worth 200-600 dollars and a old gaming PC with hand me down parts and a Nintendo switch 2. And SSI (Schizobux) money.

I don't want to lose my pets or my gaming pc or my switch 2. I am on SSI and I think the wages cannot be garnished

To keep what I have left do I just ignore the debt collectors, let them sue me and ignore them because my only income can't be garnished? I'd rather go to jail than lose my pets and my steam account and my switch 2.

It's unlikely I will get a job through what doctors call severe schizo hallucinations and delusions to pay this stuff back. The longest I've worked consistently before through the hallucination stuff is 3-4 months then quit because it got too distressing to deal with.

I can't make the minimum payments with SSI. So I need bankruptcy, my family also can't afford to pay me. I was completely non-functional schizo (didn't brush my teeth and lived in the woods or at the beach homeless for 4 years) and my life got ruined, I think what is wrong with me is a spirit fuckin with me not schizo but I can't bring up demons or spirits in court.

Early replies (first 3)

62148662
The libtards over on reddit are saying it's your choice, but if you have the energy and time filing for bankruptcy sounds like it will leave your credit cleaner after the 7 year period. The libtards are also saying there's probably free legal help you can get. Without being a lawyer I can imagine if its unregistered and relatively low value like your games and pets it's pretty unfeasible it can in any way be repossessed. Just don't declare it anywhere, it's not like expensive artwork or collectibles.

62148665
>>62148566
I am god. Listen to my advice. I have not forsaken you, my son.
You need to piss into a cup and drink it, immediately.

62148725
>>62148662
What the difference in just letting the debt go to court and declaring bankrupcy? Excuse my TLDR I don't want to vent to people IRL about this except my mom.

I don't even want to risk my pets and gaming stuff being taken because I know it's technically property even if my pets are family to me and my gaming stuff is some of the only happiness I have right now.

I read it's unlikely they'll take pets or gaming stuff but possible. That's a risk.

Working is only possible with a mananger who is tolerant of potential constant small mistakes and odd behavior if I can push through literal rape hallucinations and deities shapeshifting into the store to test me which happened before. The voices said to ask my manager if i was literally in Hell and said the voices were an angel so I felt i was supposed to even though it'd be humiliating but instead I just said mental illness symptomsand quit.

I don't want to work through what I literally confused as genuine Hell before due to angel voices. Also I'd lose medical insurance if I worked, happened before they took away my SSI due to working part time but lukcily I got it back because I ended up homeless after quitting the job.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62148734
>>62148725
>>62148566
gay pajeet begging thread.

62148749
>>62148665
this is unironically good advice if you have the quality of life to sustain higher quality pisses than most people take, it's unironically the precursor to making philosopher's stone
>t. used to break personal bests after drinking piss in the morning at the gym

62148754
They're not going to take your pets or gaming pc or switch 2 to pawn off to pay debt collectors OP.
You need to be honest with your lawyer if you have one. He can't do his job if you're lying to him.
Look up "bankruptcy personal property exemptions" you get something like $10k in personal property that can't be touched, and one car is another separate exemption. But it varies state by state.

>>62148725
>I read it's unlikely they'll take pets or gaming stuff but possible. That's a risk.
Then tell your lawyer that's your priority and he'll work to make sure you keep at least that, possibly at the cost of something else you have that might be valuable but you don't personally value at all (clearly).

Finally anon you need to actually take your meds. Hallucinations like that aren't fucking normal, and meds help. You need to just take them.

62148756
>>62148725
nobody wants to pawn your shit.
if you're serious about an heroing, offload as much of your stuff yourself as you can. it'll actually uplift you by keeping you busy. any money you can make feels like a tiny bit of progress. if you have any medical insurance left, try to get your meds straightened out. I felt so much better when they fixed my ADHD meds, suddenly everything was much easier. I'm still broke and going to the end but it makes the daily grind tolerable. Don't forget rollover IRAs and 401ks if you have them to put a loved one down as your beneficiary if you want to give that money. I did that but maybe take the 10% penalty if I feel like staying longer we'll see how the next fake interview goes

62148866
>>62148566
You need to start living below your means now and saving whatever you can. At least you got the schizobucks as a safety net, many people would kill to have even that. Put whatever you can save into Chainlink or something. You can survive on rice and beans for very little money if you buy them in bulk and with very little time investment if you cook them in bulk. Bean and rice burritos are goated poverty food. You can invest into good spices and some veggies like cilantro. Splurge on meat good cheese occasionally. But you will never make it if you don't get your mental sorted. As soon as you get your finances stable, you need to focus on mental health. If meds work for you, you need to be compliant w/ them; the risk is too high that you have another episode and burn everything down again. If your debts are way to high (like multiple years of income) I agree: fuck it, dont pay em. I know nothing about the bankruptcy process though desu. Also rec watching Eastern Orthodox videos on youtube and asking God to help you. GL anon.

62148899
>>62148866
>I agree: fuck it, dont pay em. I know nothing about the bankruptcy process though desu. Also rec watching Eastern Orthodox videos on youtube and asking God to help you. GL anon.
Anon you need to take your meds, too.

62148984
I am on meds and the only difference I notice on them is I don't have angry outbursts of behavior but hallucinations don't go away. I'm not willing to try more different meds, tried 7 already and those attempts made me obese. It took homelesness off meds to shed the weight, getting back on a low weight gaining med still made me 20 lbs overweight but its better than 80 lbs overweight.

The meds not working supports my belief it's a real spirit attacking me in my mind. I know in my heart its a real spirit but

I'll look into a lawyer maybe because of the exemption. I don't have over 10k in property.

I have

>Pets and pet enclosures
>Bed
>TV
>TV Stand
>Computer Desk
>old gaming PC that can play games on medium to high at only 60 FPS
>nintendo switch 2
>nintendo 3ds
>destroyed all physical games when hallucinating and only have digital games now
>like 12 cheap hanes t shirts, 5 pairs of amazon budget denim jeans and 2 jackets

I cant drive safely and dont even own a car. I live with my mom and she only charges me 300 a month in rent or i'd be homeless, i dont even have access to my bank account or credit card accounts because I changed the passwords when experiencing an episode and dont remember them or even what credit cards I had. waiting for collections letters to come in the mail.

Who knows about the exception in detail. Is it locked down legally I get to keep some property? Like,a guarantee legally?

I was 6k -8k in debt and I think with interest over the 4 years I was too unfunctional to even brush my teeth it went up substantially. I'd imagine it could be in the 10-20k range now but I dont know.


TLDR: Does anyone know in detail about the excemption for property in california when declaring bankrupcy?

62149203
cant tell if this is a troll, but ill bite bc im laughing so hard it hurts.

no one is taking (or wants) your pets bro. also consumer electronics are nothing. honestly, if I were you id apply for any line of credit I could. seriously just try anything and see if they give you any purchasing power. even if its only a few hundred go buy silver coins on credit and don't pay it back.

lmao at your lizards.

62149245
>>62149203
Why do you think I am trolling? I ruined my life during a spiritual attack doctors call schizophrenic episode. That's what happens during spiritual attack or schizophrenic episode, bizzare forms of life ruining.

They may not want my lizards but creditors are greedy pieces of shit potentially that don't give a shit about what I care about and love and just want money. They may not give two shits about taking my family members (pets) to get their money. I don't think I'm being paranoid.

62149284
>>62148899
Anon did not say how much debt he has or how much he makes on schizobucks. Obviously he should try to haggle down and pay it if off if possible.

62149331
>>62149245
Think about this for a second spaz. Bankruptcy procedures are typically used for businesses or people with complicated net worth & assets. Declaring personal bankruptcy is small beans compared to how it normally gets used. No assessor is coming to take inventory of your blender and lawnmower you fucking dork

62149373
>>62149284
I was around 6k in debt from memory but I dont know what it went up to with interest, I didn't check it for 4 years when completely disabled to the point i was homeless avoiding the voices commands.

I get 1260 or so with schizobux. 300 goes to rent, 120 to internet and phone. Then I spend like 80-100 on food a week.

That leave 840 for food and other expenses. 400-500 a month on food depending on if it's a 4 or 5 week month. Then I have 340 a month for anything else including taking care of pets. I also smoke cigarettes.

I'm thinking bankrupcy will be preferable to not smoking if i dont lose my pets and gaming pc and gaming console and TV

I'd literally have to have nothing at all for 7-10 years to pay it back on the minimum payment if they allowed 200-300 a month in payments total.

Bankrupcy seems better if i get to keep pets gaming console gaming pc and TV, because I'm just going to give my pets and shit to a relative or friend if they come for them. They arent getting my pets

62149381
>>62149331
I think creditors are greedy and may try to get every fucking penny personally, I don't know if they will for sure but it seems like a real risk to me

62149458
>>62149373
Well hopefully you can find some simple job that can supplement the schizo bucks, if they allow that. Don't smoke anon; you know how expensive nicotine addiction is. it just weakens your self control and is bad for mental health. I quit by switching to Zyns for some time, which i found much easier to quit than cigs, but still hard. Like i said, i don't know jack shit about bankruptcy but it does seem like the play if you would need to essentially live through 7+ years of slavery to pay it off. It doesn't seem like you are in a position to be doing any big borrowing anytime soon anyway, which is the only downside, no?

62149475
>>62148566

File for disability.

62149489
>>62149475
It's not enough to pay back debts, it's not even enough to pay for more rent than the generous $300 a month bill my mom gives me. I get 1260 or so a month for formal schizophrenia.

62149496
>>62149458
The symptoms make working even a simple job hard. It's hallucinations of rape which feels like real touch or a real spirit raping me.

But yeah 7+ years of slavery while working through rape hallucination type stuff doesn't seem worth it to not declare bankruptcy.That path seems like utter pain. Not going to do it. I think credit is ruined for 6-7 years from bankrupcy or something. Not sure.

62149502
>>62149496
>>62149489
You got enough money, fuck off richfaggot schizo quit begging.
Go sell your plasma.

62149515
>>62149496
I know you said that you don't want to change your meds around, but i don't think you can ever live a happy life having rape hallucinations, that is not okay bro. IDC if you have to go on a restrictive diet, you need to get your mental health addressed or else you NGMI even if you were rich. I would imagine even obesity is better than rape hallucinations.

62149516
>>62149502
I'm not begging anyone for fucking money. I was talking about bankruptcy.

62149532
>>62149516
Fuck off you stupid indian faggot I know who you are, post your hands now

62149774
>get fat
>blame meds
No dude, just count your calories. Take your meds.

62149783
>>62148566
Nigga Just bury your gaming pc before declaring bankruptcy. No one's gonna confiscate your lizards and sell them for $200

62149791
>>62149774
Clearly when I take the meds and eat 2 healthy meals a day I am 220ish pounds at 5 7 but when I'm homeless off meds and eating Jack in the Box 2-3 times a day I go down to 150 lbs or so, my normal weight not working out it's not the meds. The meds cause weight gain. Liars like you and doctors lying to my face through their fucking teeth to encourage me to take my meds, makes me stop taking them. The fucking paper from the fucking company says the meds cause weight gain. Lying to people with what doctors call schizophrenia to encourage or manipulate us into doing what you want us to do is what contributes to us not taking our meds. You don't know what's best for us.

I already fucking take meds but no on believes me because I still have rape hallucinations. It says online 25-50% of people don't respond to medicines if it's schizo. This makes it either a real spirit or medicine resistant psychosis from schizophrenia where i cannot tell it's schizophrenia because in my heart i believe it's a real spirit fucking with me genuinly.

62149795
>>62149791
FUCK OFF FAGGOT SCAMMER sitting waiting to respond to his bullshit samefagged thread
Go eat your lizards you pajeet begger.

62149800
Schizophrenia is just a symptom of having to deal in the real world. Goycattle can't take the strain.

item.title: PE is fundamentally broken and only serve to rob businesses of profit while saddling debt onto its owners. — 3 replies — 11.2/hr
item.content:encoded:
PE is fundamentally broken and only serve to rob businesses of profit while saddling debt onto its owners.

Open thread • Replies: 3 • Updated: 2026-04-23 21:49 UTC

OP

PE is fundamentally broken and only serve to rob businesses of profit while saddling debt onto its owners. LBOs at least contained the contamination of failing assets but now NAV debt has opened up the entire food chain as collateral. This leads to extended periods of time where exit is not available for these firms leading them to passing off the repayment to consumer prices. AI has already seen this happen with the experimentation of locking new users out of old price models in favor of doubled prices for the same product.

Early replies (first 3)

62149742
I love watching goycattle try to figure out how things work.

62149753
>>62149742
Me when I work at BlackRock and buy your grandma’s nursing home just to fire the nursing staff and let your grandma fend for herself as I restructure our annual expenditures to cut off grandma’s gas and electricity. That way I can take my exit package and resell the original business back to its owner with all the included debt I’ve accumulated in the restructuring.

62149760
>>62149753
Oh don’t forget I kill grandma with a gun and then turn Shady Oaks retirement into a data center because that’s the most efficient use of the real estate asset.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

No additional replies in the latest window.

item.title: Arbitrum revealed itself to be a CEX in chain form — 36 replies — 79.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Arbitrum revealed itself to be a CEX in chain form

Open thread • Replies: 36 • Updated: 2026-04-23 21:38 UTC

OP

https://x.com/arbitrum/status/2046435443680346189?s=20

They had the power all along to freeze any address's funds and take their crypto. Can't even cope with "not your keys, not your crypto" here.

Early replies (first 3)

62138208
>>62138188
My crypto is on ETH, so its my keys & my crypto

62138253
>>62138188
All L2s are centralized, this isnt news.

62138290
>>62138253
This is a different story in that this L2 has the power to spoof your private key. That's NEVER in the discussion of discussing if an L2 is centralized. When L2 and centralization is discussed, it revolves around the ordering of transactions and who controls the nodes.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62138917
>>62138290
>to spoof your private key
no lol. this is very different

62138931
>>62138910
>>62138917

So what is it then if I run a chain and can take possession of your ETH or whatever coin in your wallet? I know USDC can blacklist wallets, is it this at protocol level?

62138967
>>62138931
>is it this at protocol level?
Yes, they can move ETH since it is the chain's native token. Moving ERC20s would be more difficult. What they can't do is impersonate the address, i.e. they cannot forge signatures and pretend to be the private key owner

62139424
>>62138967
What else are they capable of doing that people should be aware of if the security council comes together to conspire against them? Being able to restrict your wallet and deprive you of your own ETH is a really really big deal.

62139448
>>62139424
No it isn't. it's been known for centuries that L2s were always centralized and now that skellyman denounced them there's no incentive for otherwise

62139494
>>62139448
The discussions of centralization were around node transaction handling. Not being able to control the the very ETH in your wallet. You are conflating things.

62139604
>>62138188
This is why I hold LUNC
the only truly decentralized chain

62139909
>>62138188
> executed a technical approach to move funds
kek, L2s are so worthless. ethereum should have never gone into that direction so heavily. it was obvious that the devs would never give up power within their own rollup.

62140324
anything kikes do is a scam

62140341
>>62140324
Let the goyim deal in the real world

62140513
>>62139424
They can do things that a typical validator set can do. This is like arguing that mining pools can conspire to fork the chain. Can they? Sure. Is there an incentive to do it? Not really.

62140566
>>62140513
>They can do things that a typical validator set can do.

And I want to know what that entails. If some entity mindhacked all the nodes in the BTC network or in the ETH network, they still can't take your crypto. All they can do is censor your transactions. To insert fake transactions and rewrite history, they'd have to go through the network to rewrite each node's history individually to recreate the blockchain. What Arbitrum did is out of the scope of what you'd expect from a validator set. So what can they do?

62140574
>>62140566
>they still can't take your crypto.
That's not correct. Blockchains are not immutable, they are democratic, and anything can change at the protocol level simply by changing the node software. There is an active proposal right now to censor Satoshi's coins, and if/when bitcoin decides to fork into quantum resistance, those coins will be de facto censored.

62140675
>>62138408
Eth is a centralized scam
EVM is a dogshit idea that only got traction because of its ability to be gatekept, controlled, and manipulated.

62141600
>>62140574
yeah but this is not what we're looking at here. they didn't fork the smart contract or anything like that. instead there seems to be a mechanism in place right now that allows the arbitrum conceal of gigafags to move user funds to another address.

62141625
I remember the chainlink schizos and how they believed arbitrum was also chainlink lmfao

62141991
>>62140574
>That's not correct. Blockchains are not immutable, they are democratic, and anything can change at the protocol level simply by changing the node software. There is an active proposal right now to censor Satoshi's coins, and if/when bitcoin decides to fork into quantum resistance, those coins will be de facto censored.

And all the nodes have to consciously upgrade to this new software version. At the very least there's still a democratic consensus at node operator level to accept such a proposal. That's still an acceptable level of decentralization.

>>62141600
>instead there seems to be a mechanism in place right now that allows the arbitrum conceal of gigafags to move user funds to another address.
Exactly. The only people who have access to this feature is the security council who WE have zero control or influence over. These guys are too weak to sing "Niggas in Paris" in front of their own mirror without breaking down due to guilt over patriarchal oppression and the lamentations of the oppressed.

(They're not on the council, but this is example of the folks in the industry)
https://x.com/PatrickAlphaC/status/2012634410764398795?s=20
https://x.com/va_joe/status/2042239851387290022?s=20

Are we to honestly believe that if the FBI comes slamming on the door, they'll be "decentralized" enough to reject an unjust order to move X user's funds?

What do in

62131731
>>62116681
>What exactly does it have that sets it apart from Solona and ETH?
Solana has a success rate associated with it's transactions (see https://solscan.io/analytics). That's kind of fucked up that there's a 10 to 20% chance your tx might not get processed, right? I've never seen a DOT tx fail. DOT doesn't even measure failed tx https://data.parity.io/parachain-stats?selection-mode=include&frequency=daily&relay-chain=polkadot&chains= because there are none. If you submit a tx it'll complete within like 6s to 30s. The longest a tx takes is when you transfer from one corechain to another. So when you bridge (use a HRMP channel) between corechains. And even that never took longer than like 40s from my personal experience. It's bizarre to me that literally up to 20% of the tx simply fail.
Another thing is that SOL literally has more "vote" transactions than anything else; https://solscan.io/analytics#txs . And vote transactions are LITERALLY validator messages to keep the network running. DOT doesn't count that as a tx at all. 60 to 80% of the tx on SOL are from validators to keep the network running. That's insane to think about that you need more tx to run the network than the users use to execute stuff. People are stupid for thinking that SOL processes 3k TPS. More than half of that is just to keep the network running. It's a literal scam number to be honest. It's just to make the normies believe that the network isn't a scam. The number of transactions is completely fake in a sense.
And again, the funny thing is that 1 core of DOT will be able to handle more TPS than the whole of SOL once either JAM or NOMT hit. Maybe even before that once the 500ms Basti blocks hit. If you combine 500ms blocks + JAM + NOMT one core of DOT will be able to probably do anywhere between 2 to 5x more than SOL as a whole and DOT has LITERALLY 100 cores and this will also probably be scaled to more sooner or later. It'll sooner or later scale to about 300 to 500 cores.

62131777
>>62116681
>>62131731
The 300-500 cores are after the network upgrades to 1000 validators from 600 and after a few more future optimizations. But DOT can then scale further by adding more relay chains. So, currently there is 1 relay chain that connects all the corechains (while they also have direct connections through the HRMP channels) and this relay chain has 600 active validators at any given time. At some point DOT could scale further by having multiple relay chains and separate validators on each relay chain and then having a "relay chain" that connects the relay chains. Sure, this would fragment liquidity in a sense but you could still transfer assets between the relay chains in probably like 30s or so... So in a sense the fragmented liquidity would be a non-problem. DOT is truly scalable while other chains simply are not. DOT covered all the problems because Gavin used to develop ETH and noticed "OMG, this won't work out" and then he left ETH in order to develop DOT. DOT is ETH done right in a sense. ETH is just BTC with smart contracts and DOT is an actual solution to all the problems that plague ETH and all the other smart contract platforms.

My friend goes

I have noticed a trend of the have-nots trying to pretend sons and daughters of rich parents “didn’t earn the money.”
Imagine thinking Barron Trump didn’t “earn his money” or Elon’s kid also.
I noticed the bottom 90% pretend it was just luckto make themselves feel better.
Like come on, even your parents aren’t not rich which means you come from bad stock.
Like okay, sure, “daddy’s money.” How come your dad is poor?
Thoughts, /biz/?

They lie so much it's no wonder people can't afford shit.

62148071
>>62144801
My family isn't poor but not rich either. I make a good wagie wage of $150k/yr in my late 30s, comparable to what my dad made adjusted for inflation, but I am still a wagie. It isn't luck. Some of it is environment, some of it may even be genetics, but assuming your parents had you and raised you intentionally it's due to their actions and decisions that you are where you are and subsequently your grandparents and so on.

I did not receive any direct financial help from my parents apart from them giving me a good classical education that was homeschool when I was little and then a private high school. That is an advantage over just sticking your kids in public school as it meant I was able to have my college education mostly paid for with grants and did much better than average there which lead to good internships and a jump start on a career.

I also have parents who stayed together their whole lives. They provide a safety net I can fall back on temporarily if things go a little wrong without having to worry about being financially rekt. While I haven’t made use of this personally a couple of my siblings have and stayed with them while getting back on their feet. What this has done for me personally however is enable me to take some risks to increase my own position without worry. I'm also involved in my local church community thanks to my parents which is where I found my wife, where I found other job opportunities, and even smaller things like just being able to get a lot of items like furniture, baby stuff, etc for free, having people to split a whole cow for beef with, baby sitters, piano lessons for the kids, stuff like that. This is a type of wealth that is unquantifiable but has a marked impact on your ability to thrive and progress. So even though I didn't get a free million dollars from my parents or anything like that, the way they raised me and the environment they put me in set me up to also be solidly middle class.

62148084
>>62148071
Genetically speaking there is spousal choice, another kind of unquantifiable factor that affects not just intelligence, but also physical capabilities, looks, etc. It's not random, it's not luck, it's a choice.

I am doing the same things for my kids. They may not become ultra wealthies, but I can at least give them every advantage in becoming little chads and making it to the upper half of wagies.

62148207
>>62144801
Technically they didn’t earn but that doesn’t mean the aren’t entitled to it.

62148298
>>62144801
BRING OUT THE GUILLOTINE

62148321
>THATS BECAUSE POVERTY IS A CHOICE

62148369
>>62144801
Sometimes its skill, sometimes its luck, most often its a combination of both. And on top of that you need to be driven by ambition (ie. have high T levels).

62148493
>>62145262
>but also success was much more nepotism & regionally based 1-2 generations ago
The location part is a huge factor that very few people talk about nowadays. Back in the day there was very little chance to leave you area if you were poor and needed to help your parents around and no internet meant that all the wealth was built up on specific cities depending on the field of work instead of being spread around a bit like nowadays.
But really another huge factor is that most people in the past didn't try to get wealthy because of a combination of religion and not needing to, since life was so easy mode that you could live very comfortably unless you were dirt poor.
There was very little reason to try and make it past the average and, even though it was easier than now at the right places (in part because location gatekept workforce competition as mentioned before), it still required a lot of effort unless you were a nepo baby, so most just found a level where they were comfortable and chilled there.
Now the entire middle class is getting squeezed out, so you either make it or fall down, and people have become obsessed about monetary wealth to the point of mental illness because of this. The average zoomer would rather risk his current standard of living for a chance to make it into a higher class than remaining the same and watching how they get slowly pushed into a lower class than they started at.
This is why everyone is gambling on stocks or prediction markets and why people go en masse into proffessions like software engineering and law where the average is kinda mid but the top 10% make crazy money, even if they don't have any actual interest on those fields of work and chances are they will make the same money as any other educated job they could have liked more.
All generations are a product of their environment when growing up, and the way the current young gens are growing up is tailor made to turn them into sociopathic gamblers.

62148782
>>62144801
Income, and deriving an "income" is largely tied to business and their desire to hire people at a 1) livable wage and 2) not hire down or eliminate positions as they have for the last 30-40 years.

Having a job does not guarantee stability or success as corporations have continuously eroded the cost of labor by a) outsourcing b) offshoring c) making 1 person responsible for 5 peoples jobs d) importing foreign labor (h1b's etc) to erode wages even further. and e) union busting to ensure that nobody in the working class can rally for better pay, working conditions, stability, overtime pay etc...

basically, for the last 40 years large corporations (usually multi-nationals) have conspired together to erode wages and destroy the working class. who are now being displaced and replaced with foreign labor, automation and robots.

62148905
I have an unrelated pet peeve of mine. I'm sick and fucking tired of millennials larping as if it's still 2015 and they're paying off student loans on a one-store apartment.

I applaud the ones that understand that they're getting old. The delusion can't last forever. It's the ones that pretend they're still rocking the boat despite having a house and a savings account on the 6 digits.

62149041
>>62144801
poor people do not understand rich people's problems and rich people do not understand poor people's problems.

there is no solution. they will always be in conflict with each other.

rich people never have or have forgotten what its like to be poor so they repeat the same regurgitated bullshit about how poor people are stupid and lazy etc.
some of it is true but much of it is also out of touch. it definitely helps to have a network of other rich people for opportunities a poor person will just never encounter in their entire lives.
access to favorable loans etc.

on the opposite you have poor people who have a bunch of prejudice about how rich people got rich in the first place and what it takes to actually stay rich and a lot of that is also out of touch.

again, there is no solution.
in general, people are too full of themselves on both sides.

62149175
>>62148905
Nigger the only thing I have six figure of is dead and yes I am still paying student loans.

62149402
>>62148905
>dis nigga over here doesn't understand arrested development from 20 years of bait and switch careers
I'm not in that boat; I have a PhD and a stable career (that pays like half of what it should), but I get what my peers are going through. Everything past 9/11 has been a downhill slide into a cratering society/civilization and constant economic downturn plastered over by the money printer. There's very few opportunities to bounce back if you get kneecapped with a layoff/accident/divorce/etc since its hard enough to get started with anything in the first place. Some of those people admittedly never tried, and they're losers; the ones that just got both barrels of reality to the face and can't muster the energy to start over I have some amount of sympathy towards

item.title: SIDE-HUSTLES: There are not only Crypto and Stocks to make money — 30 replies — 9.2/hr
item.content:encoded:
SIDE-HUSTLES: There are not only Crypto and Stocks to make money

Open thread • Replies: 30 • Updated: 2026-04-23 18:49 UTC

OP

Ladies and Gents,

This market sucks. The governments (nearly worldwide) suck. Here in Germany these stupid idiots are making everything worse day by day. It is up to us to create Wealth, via Crypto and Stocks BUT ALSO other venues. Ths is a Thread for ideas, proven Side-Hustles and more. Let me begin:
- RE-SELLING: I Re-Sell electronics like Consoles, Hi-Fi stuff and sometimes Vintage products. I made good money with PS4s and believe it or not Printers. You get those thrown at you and you can sell it easily for a profit.

- Business Ideas: There are interesting Business Ideas that I am trying to get into which are not Time-Intensive. For example: Washing-Machines for rent! Buy washing-machines, rent them out and make a profit with that. And many more!

What are your Side-Hustles/Business Ideas besides Crypto and Stocks?

Early replies (first 3)

62147137
Shirts

62147140
>>62147136
i want to buy a used CNC machine (vertical mill) for hobby use and also to run jobs to help pay for my costs.

62147168
>>62147137
Seems to be a nice thing - BUT: In which way? Lets say you buy that machine, then you have a couple of options. Do you want to create Logos/Names, do you want people to be able to print their own Logos with your machine?

>>62147140
Had to Google what it is, kek. Thats interesting, so it creates spare parts for machines, did I understand it correctly?

An interesting thought because some Anon posted this in a Thread a while ago: He bought Washing Machines and then stripped it for parts basically. And then sold the parts online on eBay for a profit. Im still thinking of doing this as well.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62147200
>>62147168
>Do you want to create Logos/Names, do you want people to be able to print their own Logos with your machine?
Not just logos but whatever words or things they would want. A lot of the women that come to me with things they want are either just something copied from etsy or just a couple words on a shirt celebrating whatever random event they're doing
This might limit you to simple designs at first but as you get more requests for things you can't do you'll figure out how. For images I can't cut out in vinyl like photos I use a service that prints photos on to vinyl heat transfers
Ideally you can go from businss to business finding out if anybody needs your services, or offer small shops branded apparel they can sell in their stores. A lot of options but you become the salesperson which is where many people, myself included, have a hard time.

62147217
>>62147136
>You get those thrown at you and you can sell it easily for a profit.
Sounds like too much work. How do you source it for a low price (I suppose from some stupid fuckers who don't know how to even google) and for what margin do you sell it? ROI does not make much sense imo and it is hard to scale.
>>62147136
>Washing-Machines for rent! Buy washing-machines, rent them out and make a profit with that
lol lmao even.

I am learning how to use ai and I am trying to sell videos and pics I generate online and make try to weasel my way into social media stuff and selling ai solutions to businesses.
But crypto and stocks will always be king, making my own modell with ai and try to trade with it soon.

62147223
>>62147136
Selling gift cards and accept crypto, like those Bitrefill sites, they make billions from it, there's a big market to be shared.

62147237
>>62147168
>Had to Google what it is, kek. Thats interesting, so it creates spare parts for machines, did I understand it correctly?
yeah basically.

62147255
>>62147217
Its not only Printers of course. What Im doing is basically this: I go on an App where there are listings and categories. I type in Hi-Fi for example, and then Filter the prices. If I see cheap Vintage Sony Speakers, I quickly Re-Search and if they are normally sold for more, I snatch them up. Rinse and repeat with different Inputs (Hi-Fi, Printer, Speakers, PS4, PS5 etc etc etc)
It does work pretty well, but Im not the only one doing it, kek. So I have to be quick and as you said, it is not easy to scale. You are 100% right with that.

The Washing-Machines rent-stuff is pretty interesting IMO - There are some Youtube Videos out there with people doing it and making lots of money with it. BUT: I need place for it which I do not have as of right now.

>>62147223
Can you explain that a little more? Never heard of this.

62147325
>>62147255
>I go on an App where there are listings and categories
You mean vinted or ebay? No way you are making more than 20 % margin with that. Also dead capital if you don't get buyers fast. You can automate that with ai btw so it scans for you based on parameters you decide. Or just write a bot but they often get blocked.

62147350
I was thinking to do door dash on a motorcycle and donate plasma. Between those two things I could make some pretty good extra money for very little effort. But this would just be to supplement my main income, or eventually to support my actual business when I make the leap.

62147354
>>62147325
It really does depend, sometimes I have big margins, sometimes not so much. I did not automate it as of right now. I also frequent Housing Clearances in my region which is really good for nice profits!
But for example I bought a DUAL Turntable from an Old family for 30 Euros. It is worth 250 Euros. I bought a Violine for 20 Euros (Housing Clearance) and its seemingly worth between 300-500 Euros. This of course does not happen every day, but there are pretty good deals out there daily if you look for them.

Mostly its Receivers I buy for between 10-40 Euros and sell them for between 50-99 Euros. I never buy products which are priced correctly if that makes sense.

How would you suggest I automate?

62147359
>>62147350

Plasma is the last easy side hustle that’s why meth heads do it

62147366
>>62147255
>Can you explain that a little more? Never heard of this.

https://www.bitrefill.com/se/en/

62147376
>>62147359
Most door dashers seem like drug addicts too. At least in my town. I like both though because I can make my own hours. No obligations. Haven't done door dash though, not sure if my town is big enough to really support it. We'll see.

62147467
>>62147366
Thank you, Im digging into it right now. How do they make money though? How is this a Billion Dollar Market as you say and how do YOU make money with that?

62147504
>>62147467
I read it in an article a few years back, 1 billion revenue per year, Probably a lot more nowadays.

All you need is to start a website and buy gift card by bulk so you get them cheaper.

If you have these skills then it's like discovering Bitcoin in 2010.

62147541
>>62147137
At least screen print them dude...

62147569
>>62147541
>at least increase your overhead and space requirements by multiple times dude

62147630
>>62147354
Sounds like too much work but I guess if you are short on cash it might help. Just no idea where do you even find open Housing Clearances. I only know about companies who do that and then sell the stuff themselves but that is usually pre picked and you will only find trash there. And something being "worth" vs someone paying it is a huge difference. Something is only worth something if you can find a buyer.
>How would you suggest I automate?
Learn how to write python scrips, you can use ai for that or set up and clawbot or hermes agent with access to your browser. If you make like 30 euro more with it then it pays for itself. Thinking about it maybe I go and set up something like that as a saas to sell to people lmao.
>>62147504
Good idea but you need a lot of upfront capital + trust and a lot of legal bs. I don't think you can make deals where you are just the middle man kind of like dropshiping because then you won't get big disscounts and your margin would be terrible.

62147775
>>62147630
In Germany there is an App, basically like Craigslist and lots of Families put the Housing Clearances on there with Pictures of what is there. I scan the photos, if I see electronics in there then I message and say that I would like to come for the electronics. Often times its just beds, old decorations or "trash" but sometimes there is good stuff to be had for little money.
Thank you for the tips regarding automation! I will look into it.

62147801
>>62147775
What is the app called I also live in pooristan. This shit is so easy to automate, ai has pic recognition so no need to waste your life time. Need some easy cash atm until my other stuff makes me enough.

62147832
>>62147801
The App is called Kleinanzeigen, Im sure you know it if you are in our yearly getting worse Country. Work with the Filters, put the Location on and put the radius to 20km. Search for "Hi-Fi" and put the Filter on cheapest first. Its crazy for how cheap you can get stuff that is much more valuable. Sometimes even for no money at all.
Try it with Electronics (Stereo Anlagen, Drucker, Plattenspieler etc.), and what Im going to start doing is going after Bikes. Good luck!

62147846
>>62147832
>>62147801
Oh and if you want to go after Housing Clearances specially then just type in "Haushalts-Auflösung" in your area. BUT: Lots of people do what Im doing. You have to be quick. I just saw one in my area with 2 great Electronics but I was too late even though it was posted 5 minutes before I messaged.

62148025
>>62147136
Draw futas and claim they're trans women. The less effort you put into your drawings, the better. I've seen that grift generate lots of likes for some reason. People will likely send you money.

62148189
>>62147136
peptide or oligonucleotide manufacturing. Especially nowadays since peptides and biohacking are all the rave. I've been researching and the whole setup would cost you about the same as a new suv/truck. Researchers need the product for experiments and they don't manufacture the stuff from scratch inhouse, and as long as it's not human or clinical research you can bypass the typical FDA/GMP regulatory hurdles. Would need a little bit of technical knowhow but I studied molecular biology in college, so it'd be half a hobby and half an extra way to make money.

>>62147140
only other based answer itt.

62148420
>>62148189
>>62147140
Actually yeah you guys are both based. This is cool.

62148885
>>62148189
Very interesting for sure. I just think here, there is knowledge needed and the country you are in is very important too. Im in Germany as of right now and the laws and regulations Im sure would not allow for a "normal guy" like me to produce Peptides with that machine.
I know that some Fitness Influencers work with Peptide Companys and they do make a lot of money!

It's over for IBM

Open thread • Replies: 7 • Updated: 2026-04-23 20:51 UTC

OP

It's over for IBM

Early replies (first 3)

62149184
IBM? The company that made punch cards for the Nazis? They're still around?

62149270
>>62149184
IBM as a company really only does business dealings, but IBM as the asset holder is still gigantic in computing. If you've ever used Linux, you've used things made by Red Hat which is an IBM subsidiary. Most things in open source are ran by employees working for IBM-owned companies, like Wayland (All of the display server equipment) Xorg (All Legacy display server), GNOME (The default desktop environment of Linux) and Fedora (The second most popular Linux distribution)

62149276
>>62149168
all SaaS is rerating to 10x earnings. the margins and growth of the past are gone.
